author: y4rr
content: ADR #663 item 8 landed via architect-mode trial (#681). Architect skill (#680) trialed end-to-end: pre-flight written + user-approved BEFORE dispatch; sonnet subagent dispatched with the pre-flight verbatim + 5 lessons + 3 tactical skills; post-implementation audit run; REDIRECT verdict on Cargo.toml syntax (optional dev-dep — invalid Cargo); second subagent applied the one-line fix; second audit APPROVE; cargo fmt run directly under named skip-skill exception. Substantive work: clip_grad_norm_ and clip_grad_value_ in ferrotorch-nn/src/utils.rs split into _cpu (preserved) and _cuda (new) per-device dispatchers. CUDA path: backend.mul_f32/sum_f32 (real GPU kernel launches via gpu_reduce_sum) for per-tensor squared-norm reduction; per-tensor scalar readback (4-8 bytes/tensor) as documented host boundary; backend.scale_f32/scale_f64 for in-place scaling; backend.clamp_f32/clamp_f64 (existing gpu_clamp PTX kernel) for clip_grad_value_. Mixed-device → Err(DeviceMismatch); non-L2 norm on CUDA → Err(NotImplementedOnCuda) (no silent CPU fallback). f32 + f64 both supported. Added cuda feature to ferrotorch-nn/Cargo.toml gating ferrotorch-gpu as optional dep. 5 new GPU-gated tests, all PASS on real GPU on this box. Verifications: cargo build --workspace, cargo test -p ferrotorch-nn (CPU), cargo test --features cuda (5 GPU tests), clippy x2 (-D warnings, 0), fmt --check — ALL PASS. ADR #663 status: items 1-4, 4.5, 4.6, 6, 6.5, 7, 8 DONE. Remaining: item 5 (distributions B), #677 (jit rustdoc pass). Architect-skill trial verdict: cycle was 1.5 rounds (pre-flight catch + Cargo.toml redirect + fmt nit) vs. typical 3+ rounds without architect — first measurement suggests the protocol shortens cycles.
